behaviors.description: Exclude out-of-stock products even when they have high relevance scores.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is the handoff between inventory filtering and ranking: a high-scoring item can leak through if stage ordering or merge logic is wrong. No real OS/network/subprocess is required, and correctness does not depend on AI/ML output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Run recommendation generation with mixed stock states and injected relevance scores across modules (catalog loader + filter + ranker), then assert no out-of-stock IDs appear in final ranked results.

behaviors.description: Respect user-blocked categories in recommendation output.
behaviors.justification: Bug typically appears at boundary between user-settings retrieval and category filtering, not just inside one pure predicate. No requirement for real infrastructure or model-quality evaluation.
behaviors.test_description: Wire user profile/settings module with recommendation pipeline, set explicit blocked categories, and verify returned products never include blocked category IDs.

behaviors.description: Products with recent browsing signal rank above products without browsing signal.
behaviors.justification: This behavior is a scoring/ranking rule for one algorithm boundary; the real failure can be caught by directly testing ranking weights/order logic with controlled feature vectors. Higher levels are not required unless testing data plumbing instead of ranking logic.
behaviors.test_description: Call the ranking/scoring function with products that differ only by browsing-signal feature and assert higher score/order for browsing-matched items.

behaviors.description: Pagination returns the correct page and stable ordering for repeated page-2 requests when data is unchanged.
behaviors.justification: Failure often arises from interaction between sort/tie-break logic and pagination query layer (e.g., unstable ordering between calls). Real OS/network is unnecessary; in-process modules plus local data store are sufficient.
behaviors.test_description: Populate deterministic fixture data with score ties, request page 2 twice through the full pagination path, and assert identical ordered IDs and correct offset/limit slice.

behaviors.description: At least one recommendation is from an unseen category (serendipity constraint).
behaviors.justification: This is a cross-module constraint between personalization signals and diversification/business-rule layer; unit-testing only scorer logic can miss failures where downstream filters remove novel-category items. No real AI/ML nondeterminism is required by the behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Run end-to-end local recommendation assembly (candidate generation + rank + diversification), with user history lacking one category, and assert final top-N includes at least one item from an unseen category.

behaviors.description: Cold-start users receive popularity-based recommendations, not empty results.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is fallback orchestration: empty user-history path must route to popularity source. This is module interaction (profile state + fallback provider + ranking), not real infra or model-quality dependence.
behaviors.test_description: Execute recommendation flow for a user with no history and assert non-empty output sourced from popularity-ranked products.

behaviors.description: Click tracking captures correct product ID, rank position, and timestamp.
behaviors.justification: Real failure appears when recommendation response metadata is handed to tracking/event persistence (ID/position mapping and clock stamping). Unit tests of event DTOs can miss mapping issues across modules.
behaviors.test_description: Serve a recommendation list, simulate user click on a known position, persist tracking event through normal pipeline, and assert stored event fields (product_id, position, timestamp) are correct.

behaviors.description: Recommendation generation stays under 200ms for a 100,000-product catalog.
behaviors.justification: This is a runtime performance SLO dependent on real process/OS scheduling, memory behavior, and actual data access path. Mocked or in-process simplified fakes can hide true latency bottlenecks.
behaviors.test_description: Run the service in a production-like process with a 100k-product dataset and measure end-to-end recommendation latency (p95/p99 as defined) to confirm under 200ms threshold.

behaviors.description: After purchase, the bought product is deprioritized in subsequent recommendations.
behaviors.justification: Requires state handoff across modules: purchase event ingestion updates user/product features, then ranking reflects changed state on next request. No explicit need for real external infra or model nondeterminism.
behaviors.test_description: Generate baseline recommendations, record purchase of a recommended product through normal update path, regenerate recommendations, and assert purchased product rank drops relative to baseline.

behaviors.description: Same user state yields deterministic ranked results across repeated requests.
behaviors.justification: Determinism failures usually come from module interactions (tie-breaking, query ordering, random seed handling) across the assembled pipeline, not only one pure function. No AI/ML-output quality dependency is stated.
behaviors.test_description: Freeze user/activity state and data snapshot, call recommendation endpoint twice via normal pipeline, and assert identical ordered product IDs and scores.
